title: Go Essentials
description: Learn Go from types and functions through goroutines, channels, interfaces, and modules.

modules.title: Basics & Types
modules.description: Variables, types, control flow, and functions.

sections.title: Variable Declaration
sections.explanation: `:=` is Go's short variable declaration. It infers the type (int) and assigns 42.

sections.title: Multiple Return Values
sections.explanation: Go functions can return multiple values. `nil` error prints as `<nil>`, and 10/2 = 5.

sections.title: Slices
sections.explanation: `append` adds elements to a slice. Starting with 3 elements and appending 2 gives length 5.

sections.title: Maps
sections.explanation: When a key doesn't exist, Go returns the zero value for the type (0 for int) and false for the ok flag.

modules.title: Structs & Interfaces
modules.description: Custom types, methods, and interface satisfaction.

sections.title: Struct Methods
sections.explanation: Methods in Go are functions with a receiver. `Area()` returns 3 * 4 = 12.

sections.title: Interface Satisfaction
sections.body_markdown: How does a type satisfy an interface in Go?

choices.B: By implementing all the interface's methods (implicit satisfaction)

sections.explanation: Go uses implicit interface satisfaction — if a type has all the methods an interface requires, it satisfies that interface automatically.

sections.title: Pointer Receivers
sections.explanation: Pointer receiver `*Counter` modifies the actual struct. Two calls to Inc() increment n from 0 to 2.

sections.title: Goroutine Basics
sections.body_markdown: What does `go myFunc()` do in Go?

choices.B: Launches myFunc as a lightweight concurrent goroutine
choices.C: Creates a new OS thread for myFunc
choices.D: Schedules myFunc to run after main exits

sections.explanation: The `go` keyword launches a goroutine — a lightweight green thread managed by the Go runtime scheduler.

sections.title: Channels
sections.code_snippet:
ch := make(chan int)
go func() { ch <- 42 }()
val := <-ch
fmt.Println(val)
sections.explanation: A goroutine sends 42 into the channel. The main goroutine receives it with `<-ch` and prints 42.

sections.title: Buffered Channels
sections.code_snippet:
ch := make(chan int, 2)
ch <- 1
ch <- 2
fmt.Println(<-ch, <-ch)
sections.explanation: Buffered channels can hold values without a receiver ready. Values come out in FIFO order: 1 then 2.

sections.title: Error Handling Pattern
sections.body_markdown: What is Go's idiomatic error handling pattern?

choices.B: Check returned error values: if err != nil { ... }

sections.explanation: Go uses explicit error returns. The idiomatic pattern is `if err != nil { return err }` after every fallible call.

sections.title: Go Modules
sections.body_markdown: What command initializes a new Go module?
sections.explanation: `go mod init` creates a go.mod file that tracks your module's path and dependencies.
